CGAL::Combination_enumerator.name: CGAL::Combination_enumerator
CGAL::Combination_enumerator.brief: The class Combination_enumerator is used to enumerate all fixed-size combinations (subsets) of a source range of elements.
CGAL::Combination_enumerator.detailed: For example, it can enumerate all the combinations of 2 elements from the source range [3,7) ( 7 excluded) which gives the enumeration {3,4}, {3,5}, {3,6}, {4,5}, {4,6}, {5,6} . The source range consists of elements of type CombinationElement and is specified by its first element and the element just beyond its last one. CombinationElement should be a model of the concept CombinationElement . Each combination is uniquely represented as an increasing sequence of elements. Thus, the combinations can be lexicographically ordered. They are enumerated in that order, so that we can talk about the first or last combination.

members.name: Combination_enumerator
members.brief: This constructor initializes the object to enumerate the combinations of k elements from the source range [first, beyond) .
members.detailed: The current combination is set to the first combination of the enumeration. 1 <= k <= beyond - first

members.name: Combination_enumerator
members.brief: The copy constructor.

members.name: operator[]
members.brief: Returns the i -th element of the current combination.
members.detailed: 0 <= i < number_of_elements()

members.name: number_of_elements
members.brief: Returns the size of the enumerated combinations (the parameter k from the class' constructor).

members.name: min_element
members.brief: Returns the smallest element of the source range.
members.detailed: (the parameter first of the constructor of the class).

members.name: beyond_element
members.brief: Returns the successor to the largest element of the source range (the parameter beyond of the constructor of the class).

members.name: finished
members.brief: Returns true if and only if all combinations have been enumerated.

members.name: reset
members.brief: Resets the enumerator.
members.detailed: The current combination is set to the first one of the enumeration.

members.name: operator++
members.brief: Moves *this to the next combination.

members.name: operator++
members.brief: Post-incrementation.
members.detailed: Same as the pre-incrementation above, but returns the original value of *this .

CGAL::Points_on_segment_2.brief: The class Points_on_segment_2 is a generator for points on a segment whose endpoints are specified upon construction.
CGAL::Points_on_segment_2.detailed: The points are equally spaced. Is model of PointGenerator CGAL::points_on_segment_2 <Point_2> CGAL::Random_points_in_disc_2 <Point_2, Creator> CGAL::Random_points_in_square_2 <Point_2, Creator> CGAL::Random_points_in_triangle_2 <Point_2, Creator> CGAL::Random_points_on_circle_2 <Point_2, Creator> CGAL::Random_points_on_segment_2 <Point_2, Creator> CGAL::Random_points_on_square_2 <Point_2, Creator> CGAL::random_selection()

members.name: Points_on_segment_2
members.brief: creates an input iterator g generating points of type P equally spaced on the segment from $ p$ to $ q$ .
members.detailed: $ n-i$ points are placed on the segment defined by $ p$ and $ q$ . Values of the index parameter $ i$ larger than 0 indicate starting points for the sequence further from $ p$ . Point $ p$ has index value 0 and $ q$ has index value $ n-1$ . The expressions to_double(p.x()) and to_double(p.y()) must result in the respective double representation of the coordinates of $ p$ , and similarly for $ q$ .
members.argsstring: (const Point_2 &p, const Point_2 &q, std::size_t n, std::size_t i=0)

members.name: range
members.brief: returns the range in which the point coordinates lie, i.e. $ \forall x: |x| \leq$ range() and $ \forall y: |y| \leq$ range()

members.name: source
members.brief: returns the source point of the segment.

members.name: target
members.brief: returns the target point of the segment.

CGAL::Random.name: CGAL::Random
CGAL::Random.brief: The class Random is a random numbers generator.
CGAL::Random.detailed: It generates uniformly distributed random bool , int and double . It can be used as the random number generating function object in the STL algorithm std::random_shuffle() . Instances of Random can be seen as input streams. Different streams are independent of each other, i.e. the sequence of numbers from one stream does not depend upon how many numbers were extracted from the other streams. At each time, an instance has a state that uniquely determines the subsequent numbers being produced. It can be very useful, e.g. for debugging, to reproduce a sequence of random numbers. This can be done by either initializing with a fixed seed, or by using the state functions as described below. A Random object is not deterministic when used by several threads at the same time, even if a fixed seed has been chosen. Implementation We use the boost random library function boost::rand48() to generate the random numbers. CGAL::get_default_random()

members.detailed: The seed is chosen based on the system time.

members.brief: creates an input iterator g generating points of type Point_2 uniformly distributed between the triangles of the range.
members.detailed: Each triangle has a probability to be chosen to hold the point depending on its area.

members.name: point_and_support
members.brief: returns the last point generated and a pointer to the input triangle used to generate that point.

CGAL::Random_points_on_circle_2.name: CGAL::Random_points_on_circle_2
CGAL::Random_points_on_circle_2.brief: The class Random_points_on_circle_2 is an input iterator creating points uniformly distributed on a circle.
CGAL::Random_points_on_circle_2.detailed: The default Creator is Creator_uniform_2<Kernel_traits<Point_2>::Kernel::RT,Point_2> . The generated points are computed using floating point arithmetic, whatever the Kernel is, thus they are on the circle/sphere only up to rounding errors. members.name: Random_points_on_circle_2
members.brief: creates an input iterator g generating points of type Point_2 uniformly distributed on the circle with radius $ r$ , i.e. $ |*g| == r$ .
members.detailed: A single random number is needed from rnd for each point.

Is model of InputIterator PointGenerator CGAL::Points_on_segment_2 <Point_2> CGAL::Random_points_in_disc_2 <Point_2, Creator> CGAL::Random_points_in_square_2 <Point_2, Creator> CGAL::Random_points_in_triangle_2 <Point_2, Creator> CGAL::Random_points_on_circle_2 <Point_2, Creator> CGAL::Random_points_on_square_2 <Point_2, Creator>

members.name: Random_points_on_segment_2
members.brief: creates an input iterator g generating points of type Point_2 uniformly distributed on the segment from $ p$ to $ q$ (excluding $ q$ ), i.e. $ *g == (1-\lambda)\, p + \lambda q$ where $ 0 \le\lambda< 1$ .
members.detailed: A single random number is needed from rnd for each point. The expressions to_double(p.x()) and to_double(p.y()) must result in the respective double representation of the coordinates of $ p$ , and similarly for $ q$ .

members.name: Random_points_on_segment_3
members.brief: creates an input iterator g generating points of type Point_3 uniformly distributed on the segment from p to q (excluding q ).
members.detailed: A single random number is needed from rnd for each point. The expressions to_double(p.x()) and to_double(p.y()) must result in the respective double representation of the coordinates of $ p$ , and similarly for $ q$ .

members.name: Random_points_on_segment_3
members.brief: creates an input iterator g generating points of type Point_3 uniformly distributed on the segment from $ p$ to $ q$ (excluding $ q$ ), i.e. $ *g == (1-\lambda)\, p + \lambda q$ where $ 0 \le\lambda< 1$ .
members.detailed: A single random number is needed from rnd for each point. The expressions to_double(p.x()) , to_double(p.y()) , and to_double(p.z()) must result in the respective double representation of the coordinates of $ p$ , and similarly for $ q$ .

members.name: Random_points_on_sphere_3
members.brief: creates an input iterator g generating points of type Point_3 uniformly distributed on the boundary of a sphere with radius $ r$ , i.e. $ |*g| == r$ .
members.detailed: Two random numbers are needed from rnd for each point.

members.name: Random_points_on_sphere_d
members.brief: $ g$ is an input iterator creating points of type Point_d uniformly distributed on a sphere in dimension $ dim$ with radius $ r$ , i.e. $ |*g| == r$ .
members.detailed: $ 2\cdot dim$ random numbers are needed from rnd for each point.

members.name: Random_points_on_square_2
members.brief: creates an input iterator g generating points of type Point_2 uniformly distributed on the boundary of the square with side length $ 2 a$ , centered at the origin, i.e. $ \forall p = *g:$ one coordinate is either $ a$ or $ -a$ and for the other coordinate $ c$ holds $ -a \le c < a$ .
members.detailed: A single random number is needed from rnd for each point.

RandomConvexHullTraits_2.name: RandomConvexHullTraits_2
RandomConvexHullTraits_2.brief: The concept RandomConvexHullTraits_2 describes the requirements for the traits class used by the function random_convex_hull_in_disc_2() .
RandomConvexHullTraits_2.detailed: Has models All models of the concept Kernel Operations The following two member functions returning instances of the above predicate object types are required.

members.name: FT
members.brief: The coordinate type of the points of the polygon.

members.name: Point_2
members.brief: The point type of the polygon.

members.name: Segment_2
members.brief: The segment type of the polygon.

members.name: Orientation_2
members.brief: Predicate object type that determines the orientation of three points.
members.detailed:
It must provide Orientation operator()(Point_2 p, Point_2 q,
Point_2 r) that returns LEFT_TURN , if $ r$ lies to the left of the oriented line $ l$ defined by $ p$ and $ q$ , returns RIGHT_TURN if $ r$ lies to the right of $ l$ , and returns COLLINEAR if $ r$ lies on $ l$ .

members.name: Compare_x_2
members.brief: A function object to compare the x-coordinate of two points.
members.detailed: Provides the operator: Comparison_result operator()(Point p, Point q) which returns SMALLER, EQUAL or LARGER according to the $ x$ -ordering of points p and q .

members.name: Compare_y_2
members.brief: A function object to compare the y-coordinate of two points.
members.detailed: Provides the operator: Comparison_result operator()(Point p, Point q) which returns ( SMALLER, EQUAL or LARGER ) according to the $ y$ -ordering of points p and q .

RandomConvexSetTraits_2.name: RandomConvexSetTraits_2
RandomConvexSetTraits_2.brief: The concept RandomConvexSetTraits_2 describes the requirements of the traits class for the function random_convex_set_2() .

members.name: Point_2
members.brief: point class.

members.name: FT
members.brief: class used for doing computations on point and vector coordinates (has to fulfill field type requirements).

members.name: Sum
members.brief: AdaptableBinaryFunction class: Point_2 $ \times$ Point_2 $ \rightarrow$ Point_2 .
members.detailed: It returns the point that results from adding the vectors corresponding to both arguments.

members.name: Scale
members.brief: AdaptableBinaryFunction class: Point_2 $ \times$ FT $ \rightarrow$ Point_2 .
members.detailed: Scale(p,k) returns the point that results from scaling the vector corresponding to p by a factor of k .

members.name: Max_coordinate
members.brief: AdaptableUnaryFunction class: Point_2 $ \rightarrow$ FT .
members.detailed: Max_coordinate(p) returns the coordinate of p with largest absolute value.

members.name: Angle_less
members.brief: AdaptableBinaryFunction class: Point_2 $ \times$ Point_2 $ \rightarrow$ bool .
members.detailed: It returns true , iff the angle of the direction corresponding to the first argument with respect to the positive $ x$ -axis is less than the angle of the direction corresponding to the second argument.

members.name: origin
members.brief: return origin (neutral element for the Sum operation).

members.name: FT
members.brief: The coordinate type of the points of the polygon.

members.name: Point_2
members.brief: The point type of the polygon.

members.name: Orientation_2
members.brief: Predicate object type that determines the orientation of three points.
members.detailed:
It must provide Orientation operator()(Point_2 p, Point_2 q,
Point_2 r) that returns LEFT_TURN , if $ r$ lies to the left of the oriented line $ l$ defined by $ p$ and $ q$ , returns RIGHT_TURN if $ r$ lies to the right of $ l$ , and returns COLLINEAR if $ r$ lies on $ l$ .

members.name: Less_xy_2
members.brief: Binary predicate object type comparing Point_2 s lexicographically.
members.detailed: It must provide bool operator()(Point_2 p, Point_2 q) that returns true iff $ p <_{xy} q$ . We have $ p<_{xy}q$ , iff $ p_x < q_x$ or $ p_x = q_x$ and $ p_y < q_y$ , where $ p_x$ and $ p_y$ denote the $ x$ and $ y$ coordinates of point $ p$ , resp.
